candidates.why: Represents the primary insurance revenue line and matches the role of total revenue/sales for an insurance company; preferable to a balancing item.
A technical balancing line rather than a true revenue measure; less appropriate than the explicit Insurance Revenue line.

variables.notes: Selected Insurance Revenue as the main top-line revenue figure.
No suitable cost of sales / incurred claims candidate was provided in the candidate list, so COGS is set to empty by rule.

candidates.why: This is premium revenue, not an operating expense, so it should not be treated as SG&A.
An administration cost related to real estate; this is overhead in nature and fits within SG&A-type operating expenses.
Explicitly labeled operating expenses for the general insurance segment; these are overhead operating costs appropriate for SG&A.
Explicitly labeled operating expenses for pension operations; overhead in nature and appropriate for SG&A.
Explicit banking operating expenses; overhead operating costs that fit SG&A classification.
Generic operating overhead category; strongly fits SG&A.
Operating expenses for health care services; overhead and appropriate for SG&A.
Another label for banking operating expenses; qualifies as SG&A-type overhead.

candidates.row_label: Provision for unearned premiums, gross, in general insurance
candidates.why: A technical insurance liability, not an expense; should not be included in SG&A.
Equity component; not itself a P&L expense line here, so should not be used as SG&A despite being conceptually related to stock-based compensation.

variables.notes: Selected all clear operating expense/overhead components and excluded revenue (Earned premiums), balance-sheet provisions, and equity items.
No research & development expense line was provided in the candidate list; XRD set to empty per instructions.

candidates.row_label: Interest expenses and expenses related to investments
candidates.why: Primary income-statement line capturing interest expenses related to investments; best available proxy for total interest expense.
Likely an interest line, but less clearly defined and may be segment-specific; secondary to the main explicit interest expense line.
Generic label but appears with no data in preview; unclear scope versus other detailed interest lines.
Represents allocation of profit to minority interests, not an interest expense.
Same as non-controlling interests; equity holders, not interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Component of interest expense specifically for lease liabilities; more granular but narrower than total interest expense.
Component of interest expense specifically for subordinated debt; narrower than the main total interest expense line.
Asset category, not an interest expense.
Asset line; not an expense.
Equity line, not interest expense.
Equity line, not interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest expenses and expenses related to investments

variables.notes: Chose the broadest, clearly defined interest expense line as XINT; more granular interest components were not added to avoid double-counting.

candidates.why: Represents a component of equity (similar to share capital), but not total book equity on its own.
Appears to be a specific reserve or component of equity; not total equity.
Portion of equity (retained earnings/other reserves), not total equity.

candidates.row_label: Total equity attributable to owners of the company
candidates.why: Represents total shareholders’ equity attributable to owners, excluding non-controlling interests; aligns well with book equity as used in PROF, with minority interests handled separately via MIB.
Total equity including non-controlling interests; acceptable as book equity but less ideal when MIB is separately modeled.

final_choice.row_label: Total equity attributable to owners of the company

variables.notes: Selected equity attributable to owners to represent book equity, with minority interests captured separately in MIB.

candidates.why: Income-statement allocation of profit to non-controlling interests; useful but the balance-sheet amount is a better stock measure for MIB.
Duplicate concept of non-controlling interests on income statement; again a flow, not the stock.
Asset category, not minority interest in equity.
Balance-sheet line for minority interests in equity; directly matches the definition of MIB.
Alternative label for non-controlling interests with same values; also appropriate, but one is sufficient.

variables.notes: Used the balance-sheet non-controlling interests line as the stock measure of minority interest (MIB).
